fields.summary: All Jenkins pipelines failing with 401 after credential rotation
fields.description: All 22 Jenkins pipelines started failing simultaneously at 8am with HTTP 401 errors. The failures started right after IT performed a scheduled credential rotation last night. Each pipeline uses a shared service account token stored in Jenkins credentials store. The token was rotated but the Jenkins credentials were not updated. This is blocking all deployments across every team.

content.text: Confirmed the issue - the service account password was rotated by IT last night but nobody updated the Jenkins credentials. This keeps happening every time IT does a rotation. Is there a way to automate this?
Build #2201 FAILED - 401 Unauthorized on SCM checkout step.

Updated the service account token in Jenkins Credentials Manager under Manage Jenkins > Credentials. All pipelines are now passing. Root cause: IT credential rotation was not communicated to the DevOps team so Jenkins credentials were not updated in sync. Fix: added Jenkins credential update to the IT credential rotation runbook. Workaround until automated: IT team will notify DevOps 24 hours before any credential rotation.
